title: westringia acifolia
intro: Westringia acifolia, also known as the coastal rosemary, is a shrub native to Australia that belongs to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are said to aid in treating various health issues such as fever, cough, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Westringia acifolia include its potential to reduce stress, improve respiratory issues, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of Westringia acifolia can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and it is also used in traditional medicine as a treatment for various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
